style: High pressing, possession-based with quick transitions. Emphasis on width from fullbacks, especially Trent Alexander-Arnold's playmaking from right-back. Strong counter-pressing after losing the ball.
weaknesses: Need long-term successor for Salah on right wing
tactical_notes: Under Slot, Liverpool transitioned to a more possession-dominant style compared to Klopp's heavy metal football. The system relies on patient build-up with sudden vertical passes to break lines. Fullbacks are crucial for width, and the number 10 role is key for linking midfield to attack.

style: Possession dominance, positional play. Inverted fullbacks tucking into midfield. Fluid attacking positions with emphasis on creating numerical superiority in key zones.
tactical_notes: Guardiola's system revolves around Rodri as the deep-lying playmaker. Without him, City lose their ability to control tempo. The inverted fullback system creates a 3-2-5 in possession. Haaland provides the direct goal threat that previous Guardiola teams lacked.

style: Structured possession with aggressive pressing. Set-piece specialists. Strong defensive organization with quick attacks through wide areas. Emphasis on left-side overloads.
weaknesses: Can be too rigid in attack
tactical_notes: Arteta has built a system around defensive solidity and set-piece threat. Saka is the primary creative outlet on the right, cutting inside on his left foot. Rice provides defensive security and box-to-box running. The team needs a clinical finisher to convert their chances.

style: Flexible attacking system with world-class individual talent. Can dominate possession or play on the counter. Bellingham as an advanced midfielder connecting play. Vinicius and Mbappé as twin attacking threats.
tactical_notes: Ancelotti faces the challenge of fitting Mbappé and Vinicius in the same system. The 4-3-1-2 or 4-4-2 diamond allows both to play centrally/wide. Bellingham's role is more advanced, acting as a de facto second striker at times. Valverde provides the engine and defensive cover on the right.

style: Classic Barça DNA with high defensive line and intense pressing. Youth development focus with La Masia graduates. Quick passing combinations through the middle with width from wingers.
tactical_notes: Under Flick, Barcelona plays an extremely high defensive line, sometimes pushing to the halfway line. This creates space in behind but is mitigated by intense pressing. Yamal at 17 is already a key creative force on the right. Pedri orchestrates from central midfield. The team depends heavily on Lewandowski for goals despite his age.

style: Dominant possession with aggressive pressing. Wide play through fullbacks with inverted wingers cutting inside. Kane as the focal point of attack with Musiala providing creativity.
tactical_notes: Under Kompany, Bayern aim to maintain their possession-heavy approach. Kane drops deep to link play, creating space for Musiala and the wingers to attack. The high defensive line can be exposed on transitions, which is an area for improvement.

style: Possession-based with patient build-up. Emphasis on collective play over individual brilliance post-Mbappé. High pressing and positional rotations in attack.
tactical_notes: Luis Enrique has transformed PSG from a star-dependent team to a more collective unit. Without Mbappé, the goals are spread more evenly. Dembélé provides width and directness on the right, while Kvaratskhelia adds flair on the left. The team still searches for a reliable 20+ goal striker.

style: Back three with wing-backs providing width. Emphasis on defensive structure and quick transitions. Building from the back with progressive passing through midfield.
tactical_notes: Amorim's 3-4-3 system is a significant tactical shift. The wing-backs are crucial for width, while the front three needs to press intensely. Bruno Fernandes operates as the creative hub, but the team needs more defensive solidity in midfield and defenders comfortable in a back three.
